controlBar.modeNotesHint: Transcribe speech to build your knowledgebase (graph + notes).
controlBar.modeConverseHint: Talk to your knowledgebase and hear spoken replies.
controlBar.enginePipelinedHint: Speech → transcription → graph-grounded answer → spoken reply, using your configured providers.
controlBar.engineNativeHint: Gemini Live native speech-to-speech (transcription today; spoken replies coming soon).
controlBar.engineNeedsKey: Add a Gemini API key in Settings to use the native engine.
controlBar.engineNeedsLlm: Configure a language model in Settings to converse.
controlBar.realtimeAgentOpenAiHint: OpenAI Realtime voice agent (gpt-realtime-2) native speech-to-speech.
controlBar.transcribeHint: Stream audio to local Whisper ASR.
controlBar.geminiHint: Stream audio to Gemini Live (native speech-to-speech).
controlBar.comparingHint: Both local and Gemini pipelines are running.
controlBar.backpressureHint: Audio ring buffer is dropping chunks from {{count}} source(s). The pipeline consumer is too slow — consider disabling Gemini or switching to a smaller Whisper model.

theme.label: Theme
theme.help: Choose the app's color theme. System follows your operating system's light/dark setting.

fields.openrouterIncludeUsage: Include token usage in streaming responses

modelGuidance.tinyEn: Fastest, but noisy transcripts. Good for quick tests.
modelGuidance.baseEn: Recommended default. Good quality + reasonable speed on any CPU.
modelGuidance.smallEn: Higher quality, needs ~4 GB RAM. Good for longer meetings.
modelGuidance.mediumEn: Best quality local option. Needs 8 GB RAM + M1/M2 or modern CPU.
modelGuidance.largeV3: State of the art, requires GPU for reasonable speed.
modelGuidance.lfm2_350m: Small local LLM for on-device entity extraction. Runs on any CPU with ~1 GB RAM.

credentialControl.savedHint: Saved credentials can check health and load model catalogs without re-entry.
credentialControl.missingHint: Add a key only when you want this provider to run or validate live.
credentialControl.draftHint: This replacement will be saved locally when you click Save Settings.
credentialControl.awsSavedHint: Saved AWS access keys can be used for health checks without re-entry.
credentialControl.awsSavedWithSessionHint: Saved AWS access keys and session token can be used for health checks without re-entry.
credentialControl.awsMissingHint: Use the default chain/profile, or add access keys for this app.
credentialControl.awsDraftHint: These AWS key changes will be saved locally when you click Save Settings.

modelPicker.customAllowed: Search the catalog or type a custom model id.
modelPicker.emptyCatalog: No catalog loaded. Type a custom model id.
modelPicker.noResults: No matching models. Press Tab to keep the custom value.

hints.noAwsProfiles: No AWS profiles found in ~/.aws/config. Run
hints.noAwsProfilesSuffix: or switch to Access Keys mode.
hints.deepgramMaxSpeakers: Caps distinct Deepgram diarization speakers; 0 = no cap (raw Deepgram). The local Sortformer engine is fixed at 4.
hints.audioDownmix: Pipeline downmixes to 16 kHz mono for ASR regardless — these only affect what the OS driver delivers. Click Save at the bottom to apply; restart capture to pick up the new format.
hints.logLevelPrefix: Applies immediately to the running backend. Persisted to settings so it survives restart.
hints.logLevelSuffix: env var overrides this at startup if set.
hints.openrouterNoModels: Add or use a saved OpenRouter key, then click Load models to fetch the catalog.
hints.cerebrasNoModels: Add or use a saved Cerebras key, then click Load models to fetch the catalog.
hints.openrouterProviderOrder: Comma- or newline-separated OpenRouter provider slugs. Strict accelerator routing writes the same list to provider.order and provider.only.
hints.endpointSavedKey: Saved key available for this endpoint.
hints.awsSavedKeysWithSession: Saved AWS access keys and session token available.
hints.geminiSavedServiceAccountPath: Saved Vertex AI service account path available.

legacy.label: Keep legacy provider order
legacy.hint: Preserves the existing provider_order field without writing a rich routing policy.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.hint: Uses OpenRouter default routing and clears any rich routing policy selected in this form.

lowLatency.label: Low latency
lowLatency.hint: Routes by latency with model partitioning and preferred latency targets.

highThroughput.label: High throughput / Nitro
highThroughput.hint: Routes by throughput with Nitro-oriented throughput preferences.

strictAccelerator.label: Strict accelerator
strictAccelerator.hint: Locks routing to the listed accelerator/provider slugs and disables fallbacks.

custom.label: Custom policy from config
custom.hint: Preserves a saved rich routing policy that does not match a built-in preset.

audioSources.scopeAudioHint: Show only applications currently emitting audio
audioSources.scopeAllHint: Show every running process / process tree

providerReadiness.title: Provider readiness
providerReadiness.help: Saved credentials are checked locally through backend commands when Settings opens.
providerReadiness.empty: No saved provider credentials are configured yet.
providerReadiness.error: Provider readiness could not read local credentials: {{error}}
providerReadiness.credentialFileRecovery: Repair the local credential store or clear the affected saved credential, then run checks again.

recovery.missingCredentials: Add the missing key in this provider section, then run checks.
recovery.errorWithCredentials: Replace the saved key or adjust provider settings, then run checks.
recovery.errorWithoutCredentials: Add a saved key or adjust provider settings, then run checks.
recovery.uncheckedWithCredentials: Run checks to validate the saved key before starting a session.

credentialHealth.title: Credential health
credentialHealth.help: Saved keys stay local. This view shows presence, source, validation state, and actions without loading secret values into the UI.

credentialConfirm.awsKeysLabel: AWS Access Key ID + Secret Access Key + Session Token
credentialConfirm.clearPrompt: Clear saved {{label}}? This removes the local saved credential for that provider.

conversation.title: Conversation mode
conversation.help: Choose how spoken audio is processed. Cascading runs the pipeline Speech-to-Text → Language Model → Text-to-Speech. Native speech-to-speech streams audio directly to a realtime model (Gemini Live / OpenAI gpt-realtime) — enabling this shows the Gemini control in the top bar.
conversation.enableNative: Enable native speech-to-speech (shows Gemini in the top bar)

diarization.title: Diarization
diarization.help: Choose how speaker labels are produced for transcripts, notes, and graph updates. Provider modes use the active STT provider; local modes use the local speaker model when it is ready.
diarization.maxSpeakersHint: Used only when speaker count is Fixed. Provider-specific caps still remain in advanced provider controls.
diarization.unavailable: The selected mode is not available for the current provider or local model state. Switch provider or download the speaker model before starting capture.

llmExtras.streamingPrefillHint: Warm the model with transcript while you speak and defer decoding until the turn ends, lowering post-turn extraction latency. Only the local llama.cpp backend supports this; other backends ignore it. (ADR-0012)

tts.title: Text-to-Speech & Speak-aloud
tts.help: Optional. When enabled, chatbot replies are spoken aloud through your output device using Deepgram Aura. The same Deepgram API key works for STT and TTS.
tts.needKeyHint: Enter a Deepgram API key here or save one from the Speech-to-Text Deepgram provider.

logging.title: Logging
logging.help: Write a log file you can attach as feedback when reporting issues. By default the previous log is archived and a fresh one is started each launch.
logging.modeOverwrite: Overwrite the single log file each launch

sessions.title: Recent Sessions
sessions.noSessions: No sessions yet. Start a capture to create one.
sessions.noMatches: No sessions match the current filter.
sessions.recoverTitle: Scan session files and rebuild missing index entries
sessions.recoverySummary: Recovered {{recovered}} session(s). Skipped {{skipped}}. Recoverable file errors: {{errors}}.
sessions.deleteConfirm: Move this session to trash? It will be auto-deleted after 30 days.
sessions.deletePermanentlyConfirm: Permanently delete this session? Transcript and graph files will be removed from disk and cannot be recovered.

reconnect.resumed: Gemini session resumed — prior context restored.
reconnect.fresh: Gemini reconnected as a fresh session — prior context lost.

error.auth: Gemini rejected the API key. Check Settings → Gemini Live.
error.authExpired: Gemini credentials expired. Sign in again or refresh your token.
error.rateLimit: Gemini quota exceeded. Wait a moment before retrying.
error.server: Gemini service error. Try again in a few seconds.

error.invalidAccessKey: AWS Access Key ID not recognized — check Settings → AWS and re-enter your Access Key.
error.signatureMismatch: AWS Secret Access Key mismatch — re-enter your Secret Access Key in Settings → AWS.
error.expiredToken: Your AWS session token has expired — refresh it via your identity provider (SSO, MFA, or `aws sts get-session-token`) and update Settings → AWS.
error.accessDenied: AWS credentials are valid but lack permission for {{permission}}. Ask an administrator to attach this action to your IAM policy, then try again.
error.regionNotSupported: AWS Transcribe is not enabled in region '{{region}}'. Try us-east-1, us-west-2, or eu-west-1 in Settings → AWS.
error.networkUnreachable: Cannot reach the AWS Transcribe endpoint — check your internet connection, proxy settings, or VPN.

storage.title: Storage full
storage.message: Storage full — capture paused. Free up space and click Resume.

demo.title: Demo mode
demo.message: Demo mode — download Whisper and Llama models to use the app without cloud keys.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.gettingStartedReArmed: The getting-started guide will appear again next time you set up.

express.title: Quick Setup
express.intro: Pick an ASR and LLM provider to get started. You can change everything later in Settings.
express.credentialActionHint: Add a key here to make the selected provider path runnable. Saved keys are reused automatically and are never loaded into this form.
express.credentialSavedHint: A saved key is available. The backend will use it without re-entry, so this field stays hidden.
express.optional: Configure native Gemini Live realtime mode
express.speakAloud: Speak chatbot replies aloud (Deepgram Aura — uses the same Deepgram key)

transcript.setupCredential: Let's make setup prove saved credentials are present before asking anyone to re-enter a key.
transcript.firstRun: And the first run should show transcript, notes, and graph output even before a provider is configured.
transcript.revisionGraph: The graph needs revision semantics so new context updates a decision instead of appending duplicate facts.
transcript.platformRelease: Capture and release checks still need to pass the same way on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

notes.setupBody: Settings should show saved credential presence and use backend health checks instead of requiring plaintext key re-entry.
notes.retconBody: Transcript revisions should drive diffed notes and graph patches so later context can update earlier claims.
notes.platformBody: Capture, providers, and release artifacts need evidence on macOS, Windows, and Linux before alpha distribution.

graph.savedCredentialsDescription: Credential presence and backend readiness without plaintext loadback.
graph.retconDecisionName: Use diffed notes and graph patches
graph.retconDecisionDescription: Prefer revisioned projection updates over append-only summaries.
graph.retconDecisionShortDescription: Revisioned projection updates over append-only output.
graph.releaseTaskDescription: Prove the alpha path on macOS, Windows, and Linux.
graph.providerQuestionName: Which provider should be tested first?
graph.providerQuestionDescription: Compare saved-key health, model discovery, diarization, and latency.
graph.providerQuestionShortDescription: Compare health, discovery, diarization, and latency.

liveAssist.questionBody: Consider checking saved-key health and model discovery before adding more setup fields.
liveAssist.noteBody: Promote the retcon/diff approach into the notes and graph projection backlog.
liveAssist.approvedMessage: Sample card approved into the preview projection only.

tokens.title: Token Usage
tokens.empty: No token usage reported yet. Start Gemini Live or send a chat message to accumulate totals.
tokens.turnsTooltip: Number of Gemini turn_complete frames with usage metadata
tokens.llmChatsTooltip: Number of chat completions with provider-reported token usage
tokens.newSessionTooltip: Finalize the current session on disk and start a fresh one. Lifetime totals are preserved.
tokens.clearAllConfirm: Clear both Session and Lifetime token totals? This cannot be undone.
tokens.lastTurnTooltip: Total tokens reported on the most recent turn_complete

transcript.title: Live Transcript
transcript.label: Live transcript
transcript.notRunningHint: Start capture and turn on Transcribe (or Gemini) to see live transcript here.

chat.title: Chat
chat.emptyPrompt: Ask questions about the conversation and knowledge graph.
chat.emptyHint: Try: "What entities have been mentioned?" or "Summarize the conversation so far"

notes.title: Notes
notes.empty: Notes build automatically from the conversation — participants, questions, action items, decisions, and key topics will appear here as people speak.
notes.stale: May be out of date — refresh to regenerate.

agent.title: Agent
agent.label: Agent proposals
agent.questionAdded: Added to graph. Optionally ask the AI for an answer.

pipeline.label: Pipeline status
pipeline.persistenceQueueTooltip: Persistence queue pressure: {{writers}} writer queue is full, capacity {{capacity}}, {{dropped}} events dropped. Pause capture or reduce live processing.

graph.empty: Start capturing audio to build the knowledge graph

inspect.noConnections: No connections yet — this entity isn’t linked to others.

language.label: Language
language.help: Choose the language used throughout the app.

onboarding.handoffTitle: You're all set — here's how to start
